---
title: ""
output: 
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    theme: null
    template: null
---
<style>
.html-widget {
    width: 910px !important;
    height: 975px !important;
    max-width: 100% !important;
}

body { margin: 0; }

/* Remove Bootstrap default padding */
.container-fluid {
    padding-right: 0 !important;
    padding-left: 0 !important;
}

.main-container {
    max-width: 100% !important;
    padding: 0 !important;
}
</style>

```{r, include=FALSE}
library(here)

# load libraries and test data
source("W:\\RDA Team\\R\\credentials_source.R")
source(here("MOSAIC", "charts", "chart_style_vars.R"))
con <- connect_to_db("mosaic")

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

```

```{r}
##### DOCUMENTATION (can skip to next section for indicator inputs) #####
############ USED FOR LA COUNTY CHART ONLY ############ 

### Requirements for chart behavior: Toggling between rate and counts
# rate_order - created automatically by script; helps put bars in the preferred order for rate view
# count_order - created automatically by script; helps put bars in the preferred order for count view

### Requirements: Chart Metadata (must be provided per indicator)
# data_source - e.g., "US Department of Health and Human Services, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), National Center for Health Statistics, Division of Vital Statistics, CDC WONDER Online Database (2016-2023)"

# data_description - e.g., "The number of infants born weighing less than 2,500 grams or about 5 lbs 8 oz per 100 live births by race/ethnicity of birthing parent. Estimates for racial groups with fewer than ten births or areas with fewer than 100,000 people were excluded to protect privacy. SWANA data includes only foreign-born birthing parents."

# exception: methodology_link is controlled by setting a global variable in chart_style_vars
# methodology_link - e.g., "<a href='https://catalystcalifornia.github.io/RaceCounts/Methodology/Indicator_Methodology_CountyState.html' target='_blank'>Full Methodology</a>"

# chart_title - e.g., "Low Birthweight by Race/Ethnicity"

# indicator_asbest - e.g., "min" or "max"

# total_rate - single variable to establish vertical plotline; total/county rate  of the indicator  

### Requirements: Chart data / Dataframe structure
# The ideal shape of the indicator table is long - with columns for group, subgroup, rate, count. It should have both the mosaic group (asian and/or nhpi and total which should include state (subgroup = total) and the RC race subgroups). It should also have Asian/NHPI as a subgroup under total group
# Ideally rates are already rounded in this table or the metadata table includes this (or we make all round to 1 decimal place)
# Ideally it or an accompanying metadata table includes the asbest value for the indicator.
# additional metadata fields include round rate unless we want all indicators to round to one place
# group - name of column with aggregate group name (e.g., Asian, NHPI, Total) - should be all caps
# subgroup - name of column with subgroup names (Chinese, Indian, etc.) - should be all caps
# rate - name of column with indicator raced rates
# raw - name of column with indicator raced counts
```

```{r}
##### Prep: Get data (must change for each indicator) #####
# Get indicator df
indicator_df <- dbGetQuery(con, paste0("SELECT * FROM v7.", grp, "_summary_for_charts_lac"))

# Filter indicator metadata
indicator_metadata <- indicator_metadata %>% filter(api_name == min(indicator_df$topic))  # use min bc multiple rows but all with same value

# Set metadata - see documentation above for examples
indicator_name <- indicator_df$topic
indicator_asbest <- indicator_metadata %>% select(arei_best) %>% pull()
data_source <- indicator_metadata %>% select(data_source) %>% pull()
data_description <- indicator_metadata %>% select(methodology) %>% pull()
chart_title <- indicator_metadata %>% select(bar_chart_header) %>% pull()
rate_rounding <- indicator_metadata %>% select(rate_rounding) %>% pull() %>% as.integer()
raw_rounding <- indicator_metadata %>% select(raw_rounding) %>% pull() %>% as.integer()

# Set total_rate
total_rate <- indicator_df %>% filter(group=="total" & subgroup == "total") %>% select(rate) %>% pull() %>% round(., rate_rounding)

# need to filter out NA rates/raw and total rate
indicator_df <- indicator_df %>% 
  drop_na(rate) %>%
  filter(subgroup != "total")

# Don't change - pulled in from chart_style_vars
methodology_link <- sprintf("<a href='%s' target='_blank'>Full Methodology</a>", mosaic_methodology_link)
```

```{r}
##### Prep: Edit data for chart (don't change) #####

# use indicator best direction to arrange "shared struggle" groups at top
if(indicator_asbest=="min") {
  indicator_df <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(desc(rate)) %>%
    mutate(rate = round(rate, rate_rounding),
           raw = round(raw, raw_rounding))
    
  # Create ordered categories for RATES (descending by rate)
  rate_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(desc(rate)) %>%
    pull(label)

  # Create ordered categories for COUNTS (descending by count)
  count_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(desc(raw)) %>%
    pull(label)
  
} else{
  indicator_df <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(rate) %>%
    mutate(rate = round(rate, rate_rounding),
           raw = round(raw, raw_rounding))
  
  # Create ordered categories for RATES (descending by rate)
  rate_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(rate) %>%
    pull(label)

  # Create ordered categories for COUNTS (descending by count)
  count_order <- indicator_df %>%
    arrange(raw) %>%
    pull(label)
}

# Create series data for RATES with colors
series_data_rates <- indicator_df %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map(function(df) {
    data_points <- map(1:nrow(df), function(i) {
      x_index <- which(rate_order == df$label[i]) - 1
      list(
        x = unname(x_index),
        y = df$rate[i],
        name = df$label[i]
      )
    })
    
    # Assign color based on group
    series_color <- if(df$group[1] == "asian subgroups") {
      base_colors[["asian subgroups"]]
    } else if(df$group[1] == "asian") {
      base_colors[["asian total"]]
    } else if(df$group[1] == "pacisl subgroups") {
      base_colors[["pacisl subgroups"]]
    } else if(df$group[1] == "pacisl") {
      base_colors[["pacisl total"]]
    } else {
      base_colors[["other"]]  # This catches aian, latino, black, white, nhpi, etc.
    }
    
    list(
      name = df$group[1],
      data = data_points,
      id = paste0("rates-", df$group[1]),
      color = series_color
    )
  })

# Create series data for COUNTS with same colors
series_data_counts <- indicator_df %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map(function(df) {
    data_points <- map(1:nrow(df), function(i) {
      x_index <- which(count_order == df$label[i]) - 1
      list(
        x = unname(x_index),
        y = df$raw[i],
        name = df$label[i]
      )
    })
    
    # Assign same color based on group
    series_color <- if(df$group[1] == "asian subgroups") {
      base_colors[["asian subgroups"]]
    } else if(df$group[1] == "asian") {
      base_colors[["asian total"]]
    } else if(df$group[1] == "pacisl subgroups") {
      base_colors[["pacisl subgroups"]]
    } else if(df$group[1] == "pacisl") {
      base_colors[["pacisl total"]]
    } else {
      base_colors[["other"]]  # This catches aian, latino, black, white, nhpi, etc.
    }
    
    list(
      name = df$group[1],
      data = data_points,
      id = paste0("counts-", df$group[1]),
      visible = FALSE,
      color = series_color
    )
  })

```

```{r}
##### Chart: Highcharts bar (don't change) ##### 
hc <- highchart() %>%
  hc_chart(
    type = "bar",
    reflow = TRUE,
    events = list(
      load = htmlwidgets::JS(sprintf(paste0("
        function() {
          var chart = this;
          var rateOrder = %s;
          var countOrder = %s;
          
          // Create toggle button
          chart.customToggleButton = chart.renderer.button(
            'Show Counts',
            chart.chartWidth - 106,           
            chart.chartHeight / 2 - 50,       
            function() {
              var showingRates = true;
              
              // Check if rates are currently visible
              chart.series.forEach(function(s) {
                if (s.userOptions.id && s.userOptions.id.startsWith('rates-')) {
                  showingRates = s.visible;
                }
              });
              
              // Toggle all series and update x-axis
              chart.series.forEach(function(s) {
                if (s.userOptions.id) {
                  if (showingRates) {
                    // Switch to counts
                    if (s.userOptions.id.startsWith('rates-')) {
                      s.update({showInLegend: false}, false);
                      s.hide();
                    } else if (s.userOptions.id.startsWith('counts-')) {
                      s.update({showInLegend: true}, false);
                      s.show();
                    }
                  } else {
                    // Switch to rates
                    if (s.userOptions.id.startsWith('counts-')) {
                      s.update({showInLegend: false}, false);
                      s.hide();
                    } else if (s.userOptions.id.startsWith('rates-')) {
                      s.update({showInLegend: true}, false);
                      s.show();
                    }
                  }
                }
              });
              
              // Update button text, y-axis, x-axis categories, and data label formatter
              if (showingRates) {
                chart.customToggleButton.attr({text: 'Show Rates'});
                chart.yAxis[0].setTitle({ text: 'COUNT' }, false); 
                chart.xAxis[0].update({categories: countOrder}, false);
                chart.yAxis[0].removePlotLine('county-average-line');
                
                chart.update({
                  plotOptions: {
                    bar: {
                      dataLabels: {
                        formatter: function() {
                          return Highcharts.numberFormat(this.y, 0, '.', ',');
                        }
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }, true);
                
              } else {
                chart.customToggleButton.attr({text: 'Show Counts'});
                chart.yAxis[0].setTitle({ text: 'RATE (COUNTY AVERAGE: %s)' }, false);  
                chart.xAxis[0].update({categories: rateOrder}, false);
                chart.yAxis[0].addPlotLine({
                  id: 'county-average-line',
                  value: %s,
                  color: '%s',
                  width: 2,
                  dashStyle: 'ShortDash',
                  // align right and negative x should match the hc() code
                  label: {style: {fontWeight: 'bold', fontSize: '0.8em'}, text: 'COUNTY AVERAGE: %s', align: 'right', rotation: 0, y: -8, x: -5, overflow: 'allow', crop: false},
                  zIndex: 5
                });
                
                chart.update({
                  plotOptions: {
                    bar: {
                      dataLabels: {
                        formatter: function() {
                          return this.y;
                        }
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }, true);
              } 
              
              // Remove the chart.redraw() call since we're now using redraw: true in update
            },
            {
              fill: '", rc_blue, "' ,
              stroke: '", rc_blue, "' ,
              style: { color: '", rc_white, "' },  // Add this so font color persists
              'stroke-width': 1,
              r: 3,
              padding: 8
            },
            {
              fill: '", rc_blue, "' ,
              style: { color: '", rc_white, "' }  // Add this so font color persists in hover state
            },
            {
              fill: '", rc_blue, "' ,
              style: { color: '", rc_white, "' }  // Add this so font color persists in active/pressed state
            }
          )
          .css({
            color: '", rc_white, "'
          })
          .attr({ zIndex: 7 })  // Add this line to put on top of gridlines
          .add();
          
          // Add event listener to reposition on redraw/resize
          Highcharts.addEvent(chart, 'redraw', function() {
            if (chart.customToggleButton) {
              var buttonWidth = chart.customToggleButton.getBBox().width;
              var rightEdge = chart.chartWidth - chart.spacing[1];
              chart.customToggleButton.attr({
                x: rightEdge - buttonWidth
              });
            }
          });
          
          // Initial positioning
          var buttonWidth = chart.customToggleButton.getBBox().width;
          var rightEdge = chart.chartWidth - chart.spacing[1];
          chart.customToggleButton.attr({
            x: rightEdge - buttonWidth
          });
              }
      "), 
      jsonlite::toJSON(rate_order), # rate order
      jsonlite::toJSON(count_order), # count order
      total_rate, # axis title
      total_rate, # value
      rc_black, # color
      total_rate) # county average
      ))) %>%
  hc_plotOptions(
    bar = list(
      dataLabels = list(
        enabled = TRUE,
        align = "right",
        inside = FALSE,
        style = list(color = "black")),
      pointWidth = 20,
      grouping = FALSE,
      pointPadding = 0,
      groupPadding = 0.1),
    series = 
      list(
        enableMouseTracking=FALSE, # removes all highlighting from hovering over bars and legend groups
        clip = FALSE,
        # add event to ensure no legend toggling
        events = list(
          legendItemClick = htmlwidgets::JS("function() { return false; }")
          ))
    ) %>%
  hc_xAxis(
    categories = rate_order,
    labels = list(
      enabled = TRUE,
      useHTML = TRUE,            # Required for complex CSS formatting (like word-break)
      formatter = JS("
        function() {
          // Explicitly wrap the text inside a fixed-width, wrapping div
          return '<div style=\"width: 100px; white-space: normal; word-wrap: break-word; text-align: right;\">' + this.value + '</div>';
        }
      ")
    ),
    gridLineWidth = 1,
    gridLineColor = '#e6e6e6') %>%
  hc_yAxis(
    title = list(text = sprintf("RATE (COUNTY AVERAGE: %s)", total_rate)),
    labels=list(enabled=FALSE), # remove labels from rate axis
    gridLineWidth=0,
    offset = 0,
    plotLines = list(
      list(
        id = 'county-average-line',
        value = total_rate,
        color = rc_black,
        width = 2,
        dashStyle = 'ShortDash',
        label = list(
          style = list(fontWeight='bold', fontSize='0.8em'),
          text = paste('COUNTY AVERAGE:', total_rate),
          align = 'right', rotation = 0, y = -8, x = -5,
          overflow = 'allow', crop = FALSE), 
        zIndex = 5
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  hc_legend(
    enabled = TRUE,
    verticalAlign = "top",
    margin = 20,
    itemStyle = list(cursor = "default")  # makes cursor not show pointer - to clarify no series toggling
  ) %>%
  hc_tooltip(enabled = FALSE) %>%
  hc_title(
    text = chart_title,
    align = "center" ) %>% 
  hc_subtitle(
    # Remove toggle for now
    # text = "Toggle groups on/off by clicking the legend labels below",
    align = "center") %>%
  hc_caption(
    text = paste("<b>Source:", data_source, "</b>", data_description, methodology_link),
    useHTML = TRUE,
    align = "left"
  ) %>%
  hc_add_theme(custom_theme) %>% 
  hc_responsive(
  rules = list(
    list(
      condition = list(maxWidth = breakpoint_maxwidth),
      chartOptions = list(
        caption = list(
          text = paste("<b>Source:</b>", data_source, methodology_link)
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

# Add RATES series (visible by default)
for (series in series_data_rates) {
  hc <- hc %>% hc_add_series(
    name = series$name,
    data = series$data,
    id = series$id,
    color = series$color,  # Use pre-assigned color
    dataLabels = list(
      formatter = JS("function() { return this.y; }")
    )
  )
}

# Add COUNTS series (hidden by default)
for (series in series_data_counts) {
  hc <- hc %>% hc_add_series(
    name = series$name,
    data = series$data,
    id = series$id,
    visible = FALSE,
    showInLegend = FALSE,
    color = series$color,  # Use pre-assigned color
    dataLabels = list(
      formatter = JS("function() { return this.y.toLocaleString('en-US'); }")
    )
  )
}

# Wrap with constrained width
htmltools::tagList(
  htmltools::tags$div(
    class = "chart-wrapper",
    style = "max-width: 1200px; width: 100%; margin: 0 auto;",
    htmltools::tags$div(
      class = "chart-container",
      style = "height: auto; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #ccc;",
      hc
    )
  ),
  htmltools::tags$script(HTML("
  // Remove Bootstrap padding immediately
  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    var containers = document.querySelectorAll('.container-fluid, .main-container');
    containers.forEach(function(el) {
      el.style.setProperty('padding', '0', 'important');
      el.style.setProperty('max-width', '100%', 'important');
      el.style.setProperty('margin', '0', 'important');
    });
  });
  
  function resizeChart() {
    if (typeof Highcharts !== 'undefined' && Highcharts.charts.length > 0) {
      Highcharts.charts.forEach(function(chart) {
        if (chart) {
          var inIframe = window.self !== window.top;
          var targetWidth;
          if (inIframe) {
            targetWidth = window.innerWidth;
          } else {
            var wrapper = document.querySelector('.chart-wrapper');
            targetWidth = wrapper ? wrapper.offsetWidth - 40 : window.innerWidth;
          }
          // Diagnostics
          console.log('inIframe:', inIframe);
          console.log('window.innerWidth:', window.innerWidth);
          console.log('chart.chartWidth before:', chart.chartWidth);
          console.log('targetWidth:', targetWidth);
          var wrapper = document.querySelector('.chart-wrapper');
          console.log('wrapper.offsetWidth:', wrapper ? wrapper.offsetWidth : 'no wrapper');
          
          // Also resize the widget container so it doesn't clip the chart
          var widget = document.querySelector('.html-widget');
          if (widget) widget.style.width = targetWidth + 'px';
          
          chart.setSize(targetWidth, null, false);
          
          console.log('chart.chartWidth after:', chart.chartWidth);
        }
      });
    }
  }

  setTimeout(resizeChart, 100);
  window.addEventListener('resize', resizeChart);
"))
)


```
